messages.content: What was KANU?
Kenya African National Union — the dominant political party since independence until multiparty politics was restored in 1991.
Who was Kenya's third president?
Mwai Kibaki, president from 2002 to 2013.
Who was Kenya's first president?
Mzee Jomo Kenyatta.
When did the British colonize Kenya?
Kenya became a British Protectorate in 1895 and a Crown Colony in 1920.
Who was Kenya's fourth president?
Uhuru Kenyatta, president from 2013 to 2022.
What was the Lancaster House Conference?
The 1960-1963 negotiations in London between the British government and Kenyan nationalist leaders that led to independence.
What was the Mau Mau uprising?
An armed resistance movement (1952-1960) led mainly by the Gikuyu against British colonial rule in Kenya.
What was the 2007-2008 post-election violence?
Ethnic and political violence following disputed elections that killed over 1,000 people and displaced hundreds of thousands.
Who was Kenya's fourth president?
Uhuru Kenyatta, president from 2013 to 2022.
Who was Daniel arap Moi?
Kenya's second president, serving from 1978 to 2002.
Who are the Agikuyu?
The largest ethnic group in Kenya, based in central Kenya around Mount Kenya. Their ancestral homeland was targeted by British settlers.
Who is Kenya's fifth president?
William Ruto, elected in 2022.
What is the significance of 1 June 1963?
Madaraka Day — the day Kenya attained internal self-governance, before full independence in December.
What is the history of Lamu?
Lamu has been inhabited for over 700 years, was a center of Swahili civilization and Arab-African trade, and retains its historic architecture.
When did Kenya gain independence?
12 December 1963.
Who was Kenya's fourth president?
Uhuru Kenyatta, president from 2013 to 2022.
What is the significance of Kapenguria?
The remote town in West Pokot where Jomo Kenyatta was imprisoned by the British from 1953-1959 during the Mau Mau emergency.
What happened on 7 August 1998?
The US Embassy in Nairobi was bombed by Al-Qaeda, killing 213 people and injuring thousands.
Who was Dedan Kimathi?
A Mau Mau field marshal and symbol of Kenyan resistance, captured by the British in 1956 and executed in 1957.
